description: A traveler who crosses the forest edge and continues inside uses 'muo'. It marks inward passage across a boundary, whereas 'mua' (LOC) locates an action already inside. The interior may also be a state, as in entry into sleep.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm' and let air pass through the nose, then open onto high-back rounded 'u'. Keep the next vowel in its own syllable as the tongue lowers slightly for mid-back 'o' and the lip rounding relaxes. Stress 'mu'.
sound_symbolism: 'muo', 'mue' (OUT.OF), and 'mua' (LOC) share the opening 'mu'. Their final vowels separate three fixed members of this preposition family. The audible pattern helps memory but coins no new forms.
usage_notes: The destination interior comes after 'muo'. The construction requires motion or change; movement that stays within a place takes 'mua' (LOC) instead.
